title: Vrijdagmarkt, Ghent

infobox.value: (Dutch pronunciation: [[ˈvrɛidɑxmɑr(ə)kt]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Dutch "Help:IPA/Dutch"); "Friday Market") is a [city square](/wiki/Town_square "Town square") in the historic centre of [Ghent](/wiki/Ghent "Ghent"), [East Flanders](/wiki/East_Flanders "East Flanders"), Belgium. It is named after the weekly tradition to stage a market every Friday morning. As one of the oldest squares in Ghent, it played an important role in the city's history.[[1]](#cite_note-Vrijdagmarkt-1)

Storme, M. (2013). [*Towards a Justice with a Human Face: The First International Congress on the Law of Civil Procedure Faculty of Law — State University of Ghent 27 August 1977 – 4 September 1977*](https://books.google.com/books?id=uWzxCAAAQBAJ&pg=PA14). Springer Netherlands. p. 14. [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-94-017-4513-0](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-94-017-4513-0 "Special:BookSources/978-94-017-4513-0").

Ormrod, W. Mark (2011). [*Edward III*](https://books.google.com/books?id=ZpFtNAEACAAJ). Yale University Press. p. 212. [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-0-300-19408-1](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-300-19408-1 "Special:BookSources/978-0-300-19408-1").

["Parkeergarage Vrijdagmarkt (P1)"](https://web.archive.org/web/20161006095915/https://stad.gent/openingsuren-adressen/iva-mobiliteitsbedrijf-stad-gent-parkeergarage-vrijdagmarkt-p1). *www.stad.gent* (in Dutch). Archived from [the original](https://stad.gent/openingsuren-adressen/iva-mobiliteitsbedrijf-stad-gent-parkeergarage-vrijdagmarkt-p1) on 6 October 2016. Retrieved 5 October 2016.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Square in Ghent, Belgium

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/9a/The_Friday_Square_-_Ghent%2C_Belgium_-_panoramio.jpg/250px-The_Friday_Square_-_Ghent%2C_Belgium_-_panoramio.jpg)](/wiki/File:The_Friday_Square_-_Ghent,_Belgium_-_panoramio.jpg)

Statue of [Jacob van Artevelde](/wiki/Jacob_van_Artevelde "Jacob van Artevelde") in the middle of the Vrijdagmarkt

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/56/Gent_Vrijdag_002.JPG/250px-Gent_Vrijdag_002.JPG)](/wiki/File:Gent_Vrijdag_002.JPG)

Terraces on the square during Summer

The **Vrijdagmarkt** (Dutch pronunciation: [[ˈvrɛidɑxmɑr(ə)kt]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Dutch "Help:IPA/Dutch"); "Friday Market") is a [city square](/wiki/Town_square "Town square") in the historic centre of [Ghent](/wiki/Ghent "Ghent"), [East Flanders](/wiki/East_Flanders "East Flanders"), Belgium. It is named after the weekly tradition to stage a market every Friday morning. As one of the oldest squares in Ghent, it played an important role in the city's history.[[1]](#cite_note-Vrijdagmarkt-1)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Vrijdagmarkt,_Ghent&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The centerpiece of the plaza is the statue of [Jacob van Artevelde](/wiki/Jacob_van_Artevelde "Jacob van Artevelde"), Ghent's *wise man* who sided with England during the [Hundred Years' War](/wiki/Hundred_Years%27_War "Hundred Years' War") and was murdered on the site in 1345. Van Artevelde had, on 26 January 1340, proclaimed in the Vrijdagmarkt [Edward III of England](/wiki/Edward_III_of_England "Edward III of England") as king of France.[[2]](#cite_note-2) This was the first formal declaration of Edward's claim to the French crown at the beginning of the Hundred Years' War.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Location

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Vrijdagmarkt,_Ghent&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Location")]

With its dimensions of roughly 100 by 100 metres, it is one of the largest public squares in Ghent. Every Friday morning, the square is filled with market stalls; a tradition dating back to 1199.[[1]](#cite_note-Vrijdagmarkt-1)

The square is surrounded with [guildhalls](/wiki/Guildhall "Guildhall"), which currently house bars, restaurants and terraces. In the northerly corner, there are two monumental [Art Nouveau](/wiki/Art_Nouveau "Art Nouveau") buildings of the [socialist movement](/wiki/Socialism "Socialism"), built at the turn of the 20th century, which currently house the socialist health insurance federation and the [General Labour Federation of Belgium](/wiki/General_Labour_Federation_of_Belgium "General Labour Federation of Belgium") (ABVV/FGTB).

Underneath the square there is an underground [multi-storey car park](/wiki/Multi-storey_car_park "Multi-storey car park") with a maximum capacity of 648 vehicles.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Panoramic view

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Vrijdagmarkt,_Ghent&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Panoramic view")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e2/Gent_-_vrijdagmarkt.jpg/960px-Gent_-_vrijdagmarkt.jpg)](/wiki/File:Gent_-_vrijdagmarkt.jpg)

Panoramic view of the Vrijdagmarkt in 2006
